name: Navas and Balsanery – circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Navàs offer a diverse terrain within the province of Barcelona, Catalonia. The region features varied landscapes, including river valleys and areas with varied vegetation, providing a range of gradients for cyclists. While not directly in high mountain ranges, the area includes hills and offers routes with substantial ascents and descents. This topography allows for both challenging climbs and more gentle rides through picturesque settings. Best road cycling routes around Navàs The most popular road cycling route is Navas and Balsanery – circular, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete, offering an easy ride through the local area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from Navàs, a moderate 34.9 miles (56.2 km) path that passes by historic Balsareny Castle and ascends Turó de la Senyera. Local road cyclists also love the Riera de Merlès – Sant Pau de Pinós Church loop from Navàs, a 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail leading through the Riera de Merlès area, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Navàs is defined by diverse terrain, river valleys, and varied vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Navàs are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navàs's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Navàs?
A: This guide features 18 distinct road cycling routes around Navàs. These routes offer a diverse terrain within the province of Barcelona, Catalonia, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Navàs?
A: The routes around Navàs offer a good mix of challenges. You'll find 2 easy routes perfect for a relaxed ride, 12 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more exertion, and 4 difficult routes for experienced cyclists looking for a significant challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Navàs area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Navàs are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996522993/navas-and-balsanery-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navas and Balsanery – circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a great option, as is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31331543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Merlès – Sant Pau de Pinós Church loop from Navàs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Navàs?
A: Road cycling around Navàs is characterized by diverse terrain. You'll encounter river valleys, varied vegetation, and areas with hills that provide a range of gradients. This topography allows for both challenging climbs and more gentle rides through picturesque settings, reflecting Catalonia's varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners around Navàs?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for a relaxed ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996522993/navas-and-balsanery-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navas and Balsanery – circular&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent easy option, covering 14.0 km with minimal elevation gain. It's a great way to explore the local area without significant effort.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes near Navàs?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11778694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle – Road Along Turó de la Senyera loop from Navàs&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial ascents. This moderate route spans 56.2 km and includes significant elevation gain, testing your endurance while passing by historic sites.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by historical and natural points of interest. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1096822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Castle (astronomical observatory)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellnou Castle Tower&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in cultural heritage, allowing you to combine cycling with exploration.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Navàs?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for road cycling in Navàs, with milder temperatures ideal for longer rides. While summer is also an option, be prepared for warmer conditions, especially during midday.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Navàs?
A: The road cycling routes around Navàs are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and the opportunities to explore the scenic landscapes of Catalonia.

Q: Are there any routes that explore river valleys or natural water features?
A: Yes, the region's location within the Llobregat river basin means many routes incorporate river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31331543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Merlès – Sant Pau de Pinós Church loop from Navàs&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through the picturesque Riera de Merlès area, offering scenic views along natural water features.

Q: Can I find routes that offer good views or scenic viewpoints?
A: The diverse topography of Navàs, with its hills and varied landscapes, naturally provides many opportunities for scenic views. Routes that ascend to higher points, such as those around Turó de la Senyera, will offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding Catalan countryside.
tips: © Balsareny Castle is located on a small hill that controls the Llobregat valley.

waypoints.name: Road Along Turó de la Senyera
